text: I feel a strange blend of curiosity and distance here. In the first space, I observe people gathered together, perhaps for a communal purpose—yet I remain on the threshold, more of an onlooker than a participant. The glass creates a barrier, both physical and metaphysical, between me and the center of attention. In the second space, I am surrounded by the vibrancy of knowledge and innovation. There is something exhilarating about being in a place that celebrates research and development, yet I am also aware of how the pursuit of progress is often showcased for show, its meanings layered and elusive. I feel, keenly, the tension between observer and participant, engagement and reflection.
